name: Flour Tortillas
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine flour, salt, and baking powder in a bowl.
2. Add shortening and mix until crumbly.
3. Gradually add hot water and knead the dough until it is soft and pliable. You may add a small amount of extra flour if needed to prevent sticking (but be careful, as this will result in a stiffer tortilla).
4. Allow the dough to rest for 30 minutes to an hour.
5. Form the dough into about 10-12 balls.
6. Dust a rolling pin with flour.
7. Roll each ball out on a floured surface until it is very thin and can be lifted easily onto a medium-hot griddle. The griddle should be lightly coated with oil.
8. Cook each tortilla for about 30 seconds, then flip and cook for a few more seconds on the other side, just until small bubbles appear. Be careful not to overcook, as this will make them brown.
9. Using a dish towel (or another previously cooked tortilla), gently press down on one corner of the tortilla with the palm of your hand while rotating firmly over it (a cupping motion).
10. If done correctly, the tortilla should puff up without tearing. Remove from the griddle and wrap in the dish towel to keep them warm and soft.
